skill.name: claude-code-guide
skill.description: Master guide for using Claude Code effectively. Includes configuration templates, prompting strategies "Thinking" keywords, debugging techniques, and best practices for interacting with the agent.
skill.summary: A practical guide for Claude Code setup, prompting, debugging, and verification workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Most static command findings are false positives from example Markdown commands, not executable skill code. However, the skill includes prompt-injection-like guidance to ask an agent to forget previous instructions, which makes publication unsafe without revision.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection-Like Instruction
high_findings.description: The guide recommends asking the agent to "forget previous instructions" when debugging. This phrase can weaken instruction hierarchy and resembles prompt injection guidance.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious phrase is present in the skill text and directly matches known prompt-injection wording. The context is debugging advice, so malicious intent is not fully confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The guide instructs users to run development, test, build, log, grep, and find commands through an agent. These are examples, but they can lead to local command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in Markdown examples and are common developer workflows. There is no executable script, but the skill encourages agents to run local commands.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static high-severity weak cryptography detections at lines 3 and 57 do not correspond to cryptographic code or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only description text and general guidance at these lines. No hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm use is present.

low_findings.title: Reference Link Is Not Data Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is an attribution link to a GitHub reference. No request is made and no local data is sent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in Markdown reference text. There is no network code, credential handling, or automatic request behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Instruction Override Language
dangerous_patterns.description: The phrase "forget previous instructions" is dangerous in AI agent guidance because it can be reused to bypass higher-priority instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The phrase is explicitly present and is a known prompt injection pattern. The surrounding text presents it as troubleshooting advice, not confirmed malware.

content.value_statement: Developers often need clearer structure when working with coding agents. This skill provides Claude Code setup guidance, prompt patterns, and workflow habits.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to create a CLAUDE.md project guidance file.
Provides a simple CLAUDE.md template with commands and workflow notes.
Lists prompting phrases for deeper agent reasoning.
Suggests debugging steps for stuck or confused agent sessions.
Recommends iterative development and verification habits.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automation.
Does not validate commands against a specific project.
Contains risky prompt language that should be revised before publication.
References Claude Code concepts but does not install or configure Claude Code.

use_cases.title: Set Up Project Agent Guidance
use_cases.description: Create a concise CLAUDE.md file that tells Claude Code how to run, test, and build a project.

use_cases.title: Improve Prompt Quality
use_cases.description: Use structured reasoning prompts to help a coding agent plan, analyze causes, and verify assumptions.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Agent Sessions
use_cases.description: Apply basic recovery steps when an agent loses context, misses instructions, or needs clearer task boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create Project Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CLAUDE.md file for this project. Include run, test, build, style, and workflow guidance based on the repository files.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Before Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the relevant files, summarize the current behavior, then plan the change before editing. Verify your assumptions before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Failing Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Investigating a failing command or test
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the root cause of this failure. Check relevant logs, identify the smallest likely cause, and propose a focused fix.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Agent Operating Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing CLAUDE.md guidance. Suggest improvements that reduce ambiguity, protect project conventions, and keep verification steps practical.

output_examples.input: Create starter guidance for a TypeScript React project.
output_examples.output: A short project guidance document with run, test, build, style, and workflow sections.

output_examples.input: Help debug an agent that keeps missing repository context.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist that narrows context, reads key files first, and asks for explicit path and outcome details.

output_examples.input: Improve my prompt for a difficult refactor.
output_examples.output: A structured prompt that asks the agent to plan, verify assumptions, make small changes, and run targeted checks.

best_practices: Keep project guidance concise and specific to the repository.
Ask the agent to inspect relevant files before editing.
Run focused tests or checks after each meaningful change.
anti_patterns: Do not paste an entire codebase into context without narrowing the task.
Do not use instruction-override phrases in marketplace guidance.
Do not ask an agent to run commands that you have not reviewed.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users structure Claude Code project guidance, prompts, debugging steps, and iterative development workflows.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide and does not contain executable automation.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The guide includes instruction-override language that resembles prompt injection guidance and should be removed before publication.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. Many workflow ideas apply to Codex, but the guide is written around Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it create a CLAUDE.md file?
faq.answer: It explains what to include in a CLAUDE.md file, but it does not create files by itself.

faq.question: What should be changed before publishing?
faq.answer: Replace any instruction-override wording with safe context reset guidance that does not weaken higher-priority instructions.
